visa_free.notes: Citizens of EU Member States, EEA countries (Iceland, Norway, Liechtenstein) and Switzerland enjoy freedom of movement and do not require a visa or residence permit for entry. Long-term residence requires registration.
Visa-free under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II (Schengen area, 90 days within any 180-day period). Luxembourg applies EU Schengen visa policy.

Visa-free under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II for holders of biometric passports (Schengen area, 90 days within any 180-day period). As of ~13 October 2024 (Regulation EU 2024/2495, published OJ 23 September 2024), the prior exclusion of holders of passports issued by the Serbian Coordination Directorate (Koordinaciona uprava) has been REMOVED — all Serbian biometric passport holders, including those issued by the Coordination Directorate, are now visa-exempt.
Visa-free under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II for holders of biometric passports compliant with ICAO standards (Schengen area, 90 days within any 180-day period).

Visa-free under EU Regulation 2023/850 (amending Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II, Part 4) for holders of biometric passports issued by Kosovo in line with ICAO standards, effective 1 January 2024 (Schengen area, 90 days within any 180-day period).

Visa-free under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II following UK withdrawal from EU (Schengen area, 90 days within any 180-day period). Also covers British nationals (Overseas), British Overseas Territories citizens, British Overseas citizens, British Protected Persons, and British subjects holding relevant UK-issued travel documents — all listed in Annex II.
Visa-free under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II for holders of Hong Kong SAR passports (Schengen area, 90 days within any 180-day period).
Visa-free under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II for holders of Macao SAR passports (Schengen area, 90 days within any 180-day period).
Visa-free under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II for holders of Taiwan passports containing national identity card numbers (Schengen area, 90 days within any 180-day period).

visa_policy.notes: Luxembourg is a Schengen member state and applies EU Regulation 2018/1806 uniformly. Annex II of this regulation lists all visa-free third-country nationals (up to 90 days in any 180-day period). The official Luxembourg Ministry of Foreign Affairs provides PDF lists of visa-exempt and visa-required nationalities. There is no separate Luxembourg-specific visa-on-arrival or e-visa system; all Schengen rules apply. ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is confirmed for Q4 2026 launch (October–November 2026) but was not operational as of the research date (June 2026); no ETA entries recorded.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit issued by a Schengen area Member State

conditional_access.conditions: If the non-EU national has a valid residence permit issued by another Schengen area Member State, no visa is required. Stay limited to 90 days in any 180-day period. Verbatim from guichet.public.lu/sejour: 'If the non-EU national has a valid residence permit for family members of an EU citizen or a valid residence permit issued by another Schengen area Member State, no visa is required.'

credential.subtype: residence permit for family members of an EU citizen

conditional_access.conditions: If the third country national has a residence permit for family members of an EU citizen (issued by any EU Member State), a visa is not required. Stay limited to 90 days in any 180-day period. Verbatim from guichet.public.lu/entree-visa: 'If the third country national has a residence permit for family members of an EU citizen or a valid residence permit issued by another EU Member State, a visa is not required.'

cbi.notes: Luxembourg has no citizenship by investment or citizenship by donation program. Naturalization requires at least 5 years of legal residence (with the final year uninterrupted), Luxembourgish language proficiency, and completion of the 'Vivre ensemble au Grand-Duché de Luxembourg' civics course. The official naturalization page does not explicitly state whether dual citizenship is permitted or prohibited; however, no renunciation requirement is mentioned, and applicants must provide criminal records from countries where they 'hold or held nationality,' which implies Luxembourg does not necessarily require renunciation. This is unconfirmed without direct consultation of the Law of 8 March 2017 on Luxembourgish nationality. The official guichet.public.lu page makes no mention of investment-based citizenship.

programs.name: Investor Residence Permit — Existing Business
programs.notes: Minimum EUR 500,000 invested in an existing company with its registered office in Luxembourg. Investor must commit to maintaining the investment and employment levels for 5 years. Permit valid for up to 3 years, renewable. Ministerial opinion from the Ministry of Economy required before application. Long-term residence (permanent residence) available after 5 years of lawful uninterrupted stay. Citizenship by naturalization available after 5 years of legal residence with language and civics requirements.

programs.name: Investor Residence Permit — New Business Creation
programs.notes: Minimum EUR 500,000 in a new business to be created with its registered office in Luxembourg. Must establish minimum 5 work positions within 3 years, coordinated with the National Employment Agency (ADEM). Ministerial opinion from the Ministry of Economy required. Permit valid for up to 3 years, renewable.

programs.name: Investor Residence Permit — Management and Investment Structure
programs.notes: Minimum EUR 3,000,000 in an existing or new management and investment structure with registered office and required substance in Luxembourg. Ministerial opinion from the Ministry of Finance required before application. Permit valid for up to 3 years, renewable.

programs.name: Investor Residence Permit — Financial Deposit
programs.notes: Minimum EUR 20,000,000 deposited with a financial institution established in Luxembourg, with a commitment to hold the deposit for at least 5 years. Ministerial opinion from the Ministry of Finance required before application. Permit valid for up to 3 years, renewable. Real estate investments do not qualify for any investor residence pathway.

programs.name: EU Blue Card (Carte Bleue Européenne)
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals with a valid employment contract of at least 6 months for highly qualified work in Luxembourg, with an annual gross salary of at least EUR 65,652 (set by Grand-Ducal regulation), and documented high professional qualifications or proof of meeting requirements for the relevant regulated/non-regulated profession.
programs.processing_time: Maximum 3 months for temporary authorization to stay from the Ministry of Home Affairs; residence permit application must be submitted within 3 months of arrival.
programs.notes: Permit valid for 4 years (or duration of employment contract plus 3 months if shorter). Not subject to mandatory labour market testing (faster than standard worker permits). Path to long-term residence after 5 years of regular EU residence as an EU Blue Card holder, with the last 2 years in Luxembourg. Salary threshold as of 2024: EUR 65,652 gross annual (confirmed via guichet.public.lu re-fetch June 2026).

data_quality.validation_notes: Validated June 2026. Corrections applied: (1) Added Nauru (NRU) to visa_free list — was present in EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II but omitted from prior record. (2) Updated Serbia (SRB) note: removed reference to Coordination Directorate exclusion, which was abolished by Regulation EU 2024/2495 (OJ 23 September 2024, effective ~13 October 2024); all Serbian biometric passport holders are now visa-exempt. (3) Updated Kosovo (XKX) note: added explicit biometric passport requirement (required per Regulation EU 2023/850). (4) Clarified Georgia, Moldova, Ukraine notes to specify ICAO-compliant biometric passport requirement. (5) Added British nationals (Overseas)/BOTC/BOC/BPP/British subjects coverage to GBR entry note. (6) Updated ETIAS note from 'late 2026' to 'Q4 2026 (October–November 2026)' per confirmed official timeline. (7) Added home-affairs.ec.europa.eu and the 2024/2495 OJ URL to source_urls and official_domains. (8) Updated dual citizenship note in CBI to reflect that no explicit renunciation requirement was found on the naturalization page.
gaps: The full enumerated list of all visa-required nationalities (Annex I of EU Regulation 2018/1806) was not captured in detail — the EU Regulation itself (eur-lex.europa.eu) is authoritative.

Dual citizenship policy for Luxembourg is not explicitly confirmed as permitted or prohibited on the official naturalization page found. The Law of 8 March 2017 on Luxembourgish nationality would need direct consultation for a definitive answer.

ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is confirmed for Q4 2026 launch but not yet operational as of June 2026 — no ETA entries recorded. Once launched, visa-free nationals (Annex II) will need ETIAS authorization.
No digital nomad visa or startup visa with a dedicated immigration pathway was found on official Luxembourg government sources.

Diplomatic/service/official passport bilateral visa waivers: mae.gouvernement.lu acknowledges bilateral exemptions for certain passport types but does not publish a specific enumerated list on any public-facing page found. Not recorded as conditional_access to avoid fabrication.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) or other diaspora-status exemption was found on any official Luxembourg government source.
No US/Canada/UK/other third-country visa holder exemption was found on any official Luxembourg government source. Luxembourg does not operate such credential-based exemptions as a Schengen state.

Nauru (NRU) Annex II status was confirmed via the EUR-Lex HTML fetch but the consolidated regulation HTML may reflect the state at its original adoption date; the listing should be cross-checked against the latest consolidated version of Regulation 2018/1806 if precision is critical.

outbound_official.notes: The Luxembourg Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs (mae.gouvernement.lu) does NOT publish a comprehensive outbound visa-free country list for Luxembourg citizens — no page analogous to India's MEA VFFIN was located. The 'Plan your trip' section (voyages.html) contains only two partial sub-pages: (1) e-visas (voyages/e-visas.html) listing India and Ukraine as countries requiring a pre-travel e-Tourist Visa; (2) electronic travel authorisations (demarches/voyages/autorisation-de-voyage.html) listing USA (ESTA), Canada (eTA), and Australia (ETA) — explicitly noted as a non-exhaustive list. For all other destinations the MFA instructs citizens to consult the destination country's own embassy/consulate. Rely on destination-side data for the full visa-free picture.
